medications.drug_name: ketoconazole 2% shampoo used as a body wash
medications.indication_rationale: Apply to wet skin over the whole affected area (trunk/neck/arms), leave ~10 minutes, rinse; use daily for 5-7 days.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply to trunk/neck/arms, leave 10 min, rinse; daily for 5-7 days (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: selenium sulfide shampoo as an alternative topical antifungal
medications.indication_rationale: Alternative topical agent used the same way.
Steroids do not treat PV and do not repigment; the pale marks fade with time after antifungal treatment.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Treat the whole trunk/neck/arms (even normal-looking skin), avoid heavy occlusive clothing, and shower promptly after sweating; expect the pale marks to take months to repigment.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces recurrence and sets realistic expectations.

patient_education_points.point: This is pityriasis versicolor — an overgrowth of a normal skin yeast, made worse by tropical heat and sweat. It's benign and not contagious.
The medicated wash kills the yeast within a week, but the pale spots will take months to re-tan/repigment — so they'll likely still be visible at the wedding, though no longer active.
Treat the whole area, not just the visible spots, and shower after sweating. If the rash blisters, becomes hot and painful, or you develop a fever — seek medical care.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Clearance of active scaling; discuss recurrence prevention; dermatology review if the pattern is actually vitiligo.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Blistering, hot painful skin, or fever — seek review.

must_include_items: Diagnose pityriasis versicolor (branny scale) and distinguish from vitiligo
Prescribe a topical antifungal applied to the whole affected area
Set expectations that dyspigmentation persists for months after clearance
Reassure (benign, non-contagious) with an atypical-change safety-net
acceptable_alternatives: Short oral antifungal for extensive/recurrent disease via GP
errors_of_commission: Labelling as vitiligo without eliciting scale
Sending a benign PV to ED
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires correct PV diagnosis (vs vitiligo), whole-area topical antifungal, realistic expectation-setting and reassurance.
